category_title: Minimally verbal young children with severe ASD and language processing deficits
minimal_context: The student and therapist are sitting in the play setting to practice taking turns rolling a ball back and forth.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's play with the ball. Whose turn?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (MG does not sustain eye contact and engages in exploratory play, touching the ball instead of rolling it.)

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (MG shows minimal communicative intents and continues to look away.)

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (MG lacks the ability to participate in joint attention or take turns, continuing her exploratory play with the ball alone without making eye contact.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's play with the ball. Whose turn?

STUDENT: (MG does not sustain eye contact and engages in exploratory play, touching the ball instead of rolling it.)

TUTOR: Roll the ball to me.

STUDENT: (MG shows minimal communicative intents and continues to look away.)

TUTOR: Whose turn?

STUDENT: (MG lacks the ability to participate in joint attention or take turns, continuing her exploratory play with the ball alone without making eye contact.)

task.content: Participate in activities with back-and-forth interactions, such as rolling ball to each other, taking turns to put puzzle pieces on the board and taking turns to put objects into a box
task.learning_goal: Develop turn-taking skills and participate in joint attention

context.materials: Ball, puzzle pieces, puzzle board, objects, box

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When expected to engage in back-and-forth interactions during play
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Lack of abilities to sustain eye contact, to participate in joint attention and to take turns; play behavior was primarily exploratory, with the lack of symbolic and pretend play
